path: zHide/Media & Files/Security Agreement For Monitoring Services June 15.pdf
text: RAC Security Services (WA) Pty Ltd Trading as RAC Security Services ABN 44 096 235 200 Police Licence No SA 45251 T 1300 360 504 F (08) 9436 5131 P GPO Box C140, Perth, WA 6839 rac.com.auR14399 Agreement for Monitoring Services Client details Installation address Invoice address Agreement term Amount $ Client/Company name Payment frequency Monitoring type Monthly Wireless 1 year Quarterly Landline/PSTN 6 Monthly Annually ABN Phone Mobile Email 1. Upon completion of the initial agreement Term, this agreement will continue on the same terms until notice is given in accordance with clause 5 of the terms and conditions overleaf. 2. In the case of line monitoring the client/company must maintain an outgoing fixed telephone line. 3. It is the responsibility of the client/company to advise RAC in relation to any changes to client/company or contact information as provided in the monitoring data sheet. 4. It is the responsibility of the client/company to update RAC in relation to any changes to direct debit or credit card arrangements, including expiry dates. 5. Decommissioning – At the cancellation of monitoring services it is the client/company’s responsibility to arrange decommissioning of the alarm system. The RAC will not be liable for any costs associated with the decommissioning or any costs relating to the continued telecommunications. Name (print) Name (print) Signed for and on behalf of Client/Company Signed for and on behalf of RAC Security Services I/we have read and understood the terms and conditions of the agreement. In the case of a Company, this acceptance must be signed by (1) Director or Secretary or other authorised person or (2) a partner or proprietor in the case of a partnership. Terms and Conditions 1. Definitions ‘Client/Company’ – means the person or persons who have requested RAC to provide the Services. ‘Decommissioning‘ – means the termination of active communication between the alarm system and the monitoring station. ‘Fees’ – mean the fees payable by the Client/Company for the Services being RAC’s standard fees for providing the Services as varied from time to time and notified to the Client/Company, inclusive of GST. ‘RAC Group’ – means RAC and its related and subsidiary companies, including RAC Insurance Pty Ltd. ‘Security Patrol Officer’ – means the person nominated by the RAC and despatched in accordance with clause 13. ‘Services’ – mean the monitoring services to be provided to the Client/Company as set out in clause 13 of this Agreement. ‘Term’ – means the agreed monitoring period. 2. RAC Security Services (WA) Pty Ltd (‘RAC’) will provide the Services to the Client/Company in accordance with these terms and conditions. 3. The Client/Company agrees to pay to RAC the Fees periodically in advance. If payment is in arrears at any time RAC shall not be bound to perform part or all of the Services. Payment may be made by one of the following payment methods: direct debit or on receipt of invoice. 4. The Fees paid by the Client/Company to RAC do not include the cost of a Security Patrol Officer response to alarm conditions, Decommissioning, taxes (other than GST),charges and fees imposed by the Government, Statutory Authorities, or the costs charged by communication carriers (‘Carriers’). 5. Upon completion of the initial Term, the Client/Company may give thirty (30) day’s written notice of its intention to terminate this agreement. For the avoidance of doubt, it is the responsibility of the Client/Company to provide RAC with written notice of termination. 6. RAC may alter Fees by giving four (4) weeks written notice to the Client/Company stating the new amount , any other changes to the Fees and the date from which the change will apply. 7. RAC reserves the right to revise these terms and conditions provided that any revision that is detrimental to the Client/Company will be notified to the Client/Company, and, in the case of detriment, the Client/Company will have the right to terminate the agreement without penalty. 8. RAC will not be liable for any costs associated with the Decommissioning of the alarm system, nor any costs relating to the continued telecommunication once the service has been terminated. It is the obligation of the Client/Company to advise any future owners or tenants of the property of the potential for continued telecommunications and associated costs should the alarm system not be Decommissioned. 9. Where the Client/Company authorises RAC to apply to a Carrier for any communication services necessary to provide the Services, the Client/Company must pay all charges levied by the Carrier to ensure continuity of the Services. 10. Pursuant to s.64 of the Australian Consumer Law (Cth) (‘ACL’), this clause applies in respect of any of the Services which are not of a kind ordinarily acquired for personal, domestic or household use or consumption, provided that this clause will not apply if the Client/Company establishes that reliance on it would not be fair or reasonable. Liability for breach of a condition or warranty implied into this agreement by the ACL is limited in the case of Services to supplying the Services, or the payment of the cost of having the Services supplied again. 11. Subject to any implied terms, conditions and warranties imposed by the ACL, RAC shall not be liable to the Client/Company for any injury, death, loss or damage including loss of profits and consequential loss suffered by the Client/Company howsoever caused, including but not limited to injury, death, loss or damage caused by the negligence of RAC, its employees, servants or agents, relating in any way to the provision of the Services hereunder but not limited to failing to follow up any signal or alarm or by non-receipt of any alarm. 12. RAC will not be liable for any loss or damage suffered by the Client/Company and caused by the RAC’s delay or failure to provide the Services due to industrial disputes, shortage of labour and materials, riots, natural disasters, or any other cause beyond RAC’s control. 13. RAC will offer the following Service in the event an alarm is activated: 13.1 Notify the contact(s) nominated by the Client/Company. 13.2 In the event the nominated contact(s) cannot be contacted, a Security Patrol Officer will be despatched in accordance with the Client/ Company’s recorded instructions established at the time of commissioning. The cost for this service is to be borne by the Client/Company. 14. The Client/Company must ensure that all their equipment which is necessary for RAC to provide the Services is maintained in accordance with the operating instructions supplied with the equipment and the routine maintenance recommended in the Australian Standards AS2201 (equipment maintained at a minimum rate of once (1) per year with no more than thirteen (13) months between visits). RAC’s obligations to provide the Services are suspended during any period in which the Client/Company is in breach of this clause. 15. The Client /Company shall advise RAC in writing if during the Term the nominate contact(s) number(s) change. 16. If a party is in breach of any of these terms and conditions and does not rectify that breach within fourteen (14) days of receiving notice in writing to do so from the other party, then the other party is entitled to terminate this agreement by written notice effective immediately. 17. On termination of this agreement under clause 16,the Client/Company must pay RAC all Fees which would otherwise fall due for payment prior to or on the date of termination. 18. The Services come with certain consumer guarantees which cannot be excluded under the ACL. The guarantees are in addition to other rights and remedies which may be legally available. 19. RAC may assign its rights or subcontract whole or part of the Services under this agreement. 20. These terms and conditions prevail over any inconsistent terms in any other document. 21. The RAC Group Privacy Policy is detailed in its website by visiting www.rac.com.au or by phoning 1300 360 504 and selecting 0 on the options. Should a copy of the statement be required this may also be requested any time during RAC office hours.
